project.name: daily-llm-viewer
project.goal: Streamlit-based analysis tool for the LLM daily test results. Supersedes _old_viewer.py + _old_ingest.py + _old_schema.sql.
project.language_preference: ko-KR for conversation; English for code/comments/docs

pipeline.viewer_consumes: DuckDB read-only via queries.py; Streamlit app in app.py

daily/viewer/schema.sql.purpose: DuckDB schema. Source of truth. ensure_schema() runs this on every ingest.
notes: perf stores RAW in_token/out_token. No bucketing at write time (per user requirement B).
perf_stats uses correlated subqueries for rolling median + MAD over a 30-day back-window. Fast enough (<1s) at 27k rows; switch to CTE/Python if it slows.
runs.rawlog_path stores a FILE PATH, not the log body (old schema stored the whole log in a TEXT column; removed to keep DB small).
runs.source_format is 'old' | 'new' — keep this for debugging data provenance.

daily/viewer/profiles/default.yaml.purpose: Replacement for FIXED_ROW_ORDER. Controls row order in Excel-paste tab.

match_spec_semantics.*: any value (token-agnostic; used for SD/LCM pipelines)

daily/viewer/profiles/default.yaml.extension: Add e.g. profiles/iGPU.yaml for machine-specific rows. Load via `python -m viewer.ingest.cli --profile <yaml>`. Sidebar picks between loaded profiles.

daily/viewer/ingest/record.py.purpose: RunRecord + DeviceRecord + PerfRow dataclasses. Format-neutral — loaders fill these, writer consumes them.
daily/viewer/ingest/record.py.do_not: Add format-specific fields here. Put them on the loader.

exports: file_hash (sha256 content hash of source file)

daily/viewer/ingest/loader_new.py.purpose: summary.json -> RunRecord. Raw token counts preserved.
daily/viewer/ingest/loader_new.py.meta_handling: summary.meta is optional — falls back to filename stamp + parent-dir machine + generated_at ts. Early summary files (before meta landed) still work.

daily/viewer/ingest/loader_old.py.pickle_trick: _TolerantUnpickler.find_class replaces missing classes (test_cases.TestBenchmark etc.) with _UnknownClass stubs. Key tuple is (model, precision, TestClass) — we read TestClass.__name__ to dispatch to the correct extractor.
daily/viewer/ingest/loader_old.py.version_parsing: OV version pulled from filename 'daily.<stamp>.<ov_version>.pickle' AND from .report text (Purpose + OpenVINO line). Report text is preferred when present.
daily/viewer/ingest/loader_old.py.memory_size_conversion: _sizestr_to_gb handles '8.5 GB' / '1024 MB' strings from qwen_usage.

daily/viewer/ingest/writer.py.purpose: DuckDB upsert (transactional per RunRecord) + display profile loader.
daily/viewer/ingest/writer.py.dedup: perf is replaced wholesale per run (DELETE + INSERT). Duplicate PKs within a run are deduplicated to last-wins.

daily/viewer/ingest/cli.py.purpose: Single CLI; --root scans, --input handles one file.
daily/viewer/ingest/cli.py.auto_detect: _classify: *.summary.json -> new; *.pickle (with sibling .report) -> old; *.report alone -> old if sibling pickle exists, else skip.
daily/viewer/ingest/cli.py.progress: Inline progress bar. Failures collected and reported at end; not fatal.
daily/viewer/ingest/cli.py.profile_load: Each run (re)loads profiles/default.yaml into display_rows unless --skip-profile.

daily/viewer/queries.py.purpose: DuckDB helpers used by app.py. All return pandas DataFrames.

funcs.list_runs: runs metadata for one machine, newest first
funcs.build_excel_matrix: Cross-join display_rows x selected runs, LEFT JOIN perf_with_buckets, then manual pivot (pandas pivot_table drops NaN index and explodes on dropna=False)
funcs.extra_rows: Perf rows in selected runs NOT covered by the display profile — QA aid
funcs.series_history: Time series of one (machine, model, precision, in_token, out_token, exec_mode) with perf_stats columns
funcs.regressions_for_run: z_score + pct_diff flags with direction sensitivity (ms/s/% -> lower better; FPS/tps -> higher better)

tabs.Excel: Select runs -> wide matrix (profile rows x run stamps) + tab-separated paste block + 'extra rows' expander
tabs.Trend: Multiselect series from latest run; plot line + median ± 2σ band
tabs.Regressions: Pick run -> regression/improvement tables; metrics row shows counts
tabs.Geomean: exec_mode x in_bucket x out_bucket geomean trend + band + latest-point alert (user-requested)
tabs.Noise: Per-series CV table sorted desc (iGPU diagnostics)

daily/viewer/app.py.caching: All queries wrapped with @st.cache_data keyed on DB mtime to auto-invalidate on re-ingest.
daily/viewer/app.py.config_source: DAILY_DB env var or `-- --db <path>` trailing arg (streamlit swallows its own flags).

files.daily/viewer/perf_rows.py: Used by xlsx_update.py path (flatten summary -> lookup dict). Kept as-is. NOTE: this one DOES bucket ('short'/'long') for the xlsx template — do not reuse for DB ingest (which must keep raw tokens).
files.daily/viewer/xlsx_update.py: Separate xlsx append path invoked from run.py. Independent of the DuckDB pipeline.

rejected: Python list in code (no version control per-row, harder to diff)
design_decisions.why: User adds/removes models frequently. YAML is reviewable in git; DB copy is for fast JOINs in queries. Match spec ('short'/'long'/'*'/<int>') is expressive enough without code changes.

design_decisions.topic: Raw tokens in DB vs bucketed
design_decisions.user_request: B — ingest is data-as-is; viewer does the bucketing
design_decisions.implementation: perf_with_buckets view derives in_bucket/out_bucket. Threshold lives in SQL — change the view to retune, no re-ingest needed.

design_decisions.choice: Robust z-score using MAD + pct threshold (both must trigger)
rejected: mean/std z-score — too sensitive to iGPU outliers
single-point baseline — flips on any blip
design_decisions.why: User flagged iGPU fluctuation as a core problem. MAD is outlier-resistant. Requiring both z and pct thresholds reduces false positives further. 'noisy_cv' flag tells the user which series are inherently jittery so a flagged regression there should be treated with skepticism.
design_decisions.ui: All four exposed as sidebar sliders.

design_decisions.user_request: C — user explicitly wants geomean-level trend alerts (not just per-series)
design_decisions.implementation: geomean_trend() groups by (machine, exec_mode, in_bucket, out_bucket). Tab computes median + MAD of the geomean series itself and shows a banner if latest point falls outside ±z·σ AND ±pct%.

design_decisions.why: Including iso_ts makes re-runs minutes apart distinct even if the file stem somehow collides.

design_decisions.choice: sha256 content hash of the source file (pickle or summary.json)
design_decisions.why: Stable across path changes / rsyncs / backups. Replaces the old (path|size|mtime) scheme which broke on copy.
design_decisions.cost: Hashing is I/O-bound but amortised: 453 pickles in 4.7s on a local drive.

design_decisions.choice: Store PATH only (rawlog_path); never the body
design_decisions.old_behavior: _old_schema.sql stored the full text in runs.rawlog — this exploded the DB
design_decisions.mitigation: If log access is needed from the UI, open the file from the path. Viewer doesn't do this yet.

design_decisions.why: Legacy pickles reference test_cases.test_benchmark.TestBenchmark etc. We don't want to import that package into the viewer. We only need the class NAME to dispatch extractors.

design_decisions.choice: One transaction per RunRecord (runs + system_devices + perf together)
design_decisions.why: Partial-state bugs in the old ingest (separate inserts) were painful. Run is the natural unit.
Streamlit cache invalidates when any cache key changes. Passing mtime means re-ingest auto-refreshes all cached queries without manual invalidation.

logic: TRY_CAST(spec AS INTEGER) = token -> exact numeric match
match_rules_in_sql.note: All three are OR'd. TRY_CAST returns NULL for non-integer strings, which cleanly fails the comparison.

how_to_run.reload_profile_only: python -m viewer.ingest.cli --root /dev/null --profile viewer/profiles/default.yaml   # or cleaner: keep an --only-profile flag if needed
how_to_run.launch_viewer: cd daily && streamlit run viewer/app.py -- --db viewer/bench.duckdb

smoke_test_results_2026_04_25.new_format_ingest: 1 file (daily.20260422_0903.summary.json from dg2raptorlake) -> 4 perf rows. OK.
smoke_test_results_2026_04_25.old_format_ingest: 1 file (daily.20250224_0104 from res/) -> 74 perf rows. OK.
smoke_test_results_2026_04_25.bulk_old_ingest: 453 files from /var/www/html/daily/LNL-02 -> 452 runs (one collision handled correctly), 27k perf rows, 4.7s, 0 failures.
smoke_test_results_2026_04_25.perf_stats_query: 27001/27283 rows have a valid baseline (others are first-in-series). 0.1s.
smoke_test_results_2026_04_25.viewer_smoke: All 5 tab render functions called headlessly against real data with mocked streamlit — no uncaught exceptions. Streamlit server starts on port and serves shell (200 OK).

known_gaps_and_future_work.item: Viewer doesn't open raw logs from rawlog_path yet.
known_gaps_and_future_work.why_not_done: Not requested. One-liner to add in Regressions tab or a new 'Raw Log' tab.
All modules were smoke-tested against real data. If the codebase grows, pytest on _common.py (stamp/hash parsers), loader_new/loader_old (fixture files), and queries (synthetic DB) would be worth it.

known_gaps_and_future_work.when_to_revisit: If query time exceeds 1s, rewrite as a Python-side precomputation that writes into a cached `perf_stats_cached` table on ingest.

known_gaps_and_future_work.why_not_done: Out of scope for this change. Hook would be: after run.py's send_mail, run queries.regressions_for_run and append a section to the mail body.

known_gaps_and_future_work.item: 'description' in runs currently copies 'purpose' when loading old format. Not sure if the distinction ever mattered — new summary.json uses 'description' from CLI args.
known_gaps_and_future_work.action_if_it_breaks_ui: Keep the two columns; in queries, coalesce them: COALESCE(description, purpose).

known_gaps_and_future_work.item: Profile CLI currently always loads default.yaml on every ingest unless --skip-profile. To use a different profile, call once with --profile <path> --skip-profile (to avoid overwrite), then pass --profile to load the desired one without overwriting default.
known_gaps_and_future_work.possible_refactor: Make --profile additive by default: skip-if-exists semantics, or always upsert only the named profile without touching others.

known_gaps_and_future_work.item: machine name for pickles ingested from non-canonical locations
known_gaps_and_future_work.why: loader_old.py derives machine from parent dir name. If a pickle sits in a dir not named after the machine, the value is wrong (seen with res/ fixture -> machine='res').
known_gaps_and_future_work.mitigation: Acceptable for real /var/www/html/daily/<MACHINE>/ layout. If we need to rescue fixtures, add a --machine override to cli.py.

units.%: percent (memory/cpu), treated as lower_is_better for regression direction — revisit if that ever flips

user_profile.role: OpenVINO GPU plugin developer; owns the daily benchmark pipeline.
preferences: Korean for conversation; English for code/doc artefacts.
Autonomous execution preferred once direction is agreed — do not pause for confirmation on each substep.
Appreciates explicit trade-off analysis when multiple approaches exist.
